data.title: Barclays continues to prefer U.S. equities over rest of the world
data.description:
Barclays continued to favor U.S. equities over the rest of the world, citing economic data surprises, a series of trade deals, and improving earnings from Big Tech.

The S&P 500 has closed its year-to-date performance gap with other regions, and even as valuations are elevated, large-cap U.S. stocks remain within historical norms. 

"Big Tech earnings are improving, but valuations are still down from YE24, a key argument for U.S. equities. As the main engine of US equity profitability for the last few years, 2Q25 results from GOOGL, META and MSFT suggest that growth momentum is intact," analyst Venu Krishna said. 

At the same time, Big Tech as a group is trading in the bottom quartile of its historical premium vs. SPX, proposing room for upside from here, Krishna added. 

The revival of U.S. confidence might also be showing in FX, as DXY is stabilizing after its record-setting first half of the year. 

"Over the last month, the USD has been paring back losses against major G10 peers, notably GBP and JPY," Krishna said. 

Barclays added that there has been a significant pickup in APAC/EM equity returns in recent weeks but cautioned that EPS revisions in APAC are trending negative, and margins are expected to weaken year-on-year.

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ).

data.title: I-Mab prices $65M ADS offering
data.description:
• I-Mab (NASDAQ:IMAB) has priced a public offering of 33.3 million American Depositary Shares (ADSs) at $1.95 per ADS, raising about $65 million in gross proceeds.
  • All shares are being sold by the company, and the offering is expected to close on August 5, 2025, pending usual conditions.
  • I-Mab plans to use the funds—along with its current cash—to support ongoing clinical trials, including a Phase 2 study of givastomig, a bispecific antibody targeting Claudin 18.2 and 4-1BB, with results expected by late 2027. Proceeds will also go toward working capital and general corporate needs.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Imperial Oil GAAP EPS of C$1.86, revenue of C$11.23B
data.description:
• Imperial Oil press release (NYSE:IMO): Q2 GAAP EPS of C$1.86.
  • Revenue of C$11.23B (-16.1% Y/Y).
  • Cash flows from operating activities of C$1,465 million and cash flows from operating activities excluding working capital of C$1,413 million.
  • Upstream production of 427,000 gross oil-equivalent barrels per day, the highest second quarter in over 30 years.
  • Kearl achieved highest-ever second quarter production of 275,000 total gross oil-equivalent barrels per day (195,000 barrels Imperial's share).

data.title: Magna International protects Q2 profits as vehicle production slows
data.description:
Although Magna International (NYSE:MGA) realized a 3% decline in sales during the second quarter due to a drop in light vehicle production in Europe and North America, the parts manufacturer beat top- and bottom-line expectations for the quarter and raised its full year guidance despite ongoing industry headwinds and continued trade policy uncertainty.

Shares are up more than 4% in Friday’s premarket trading.

For the quarter ended June 30, the company earned a profit of $1.44 per share, up 7% from the same quarter last year and $0.29 better than anticipated thanks to cost efficiency and restructuring measures.

And while sales were modestly lower at $10.63B due to lower vehicle production and end of production for the Jaguar I-Pace and E-Pace, it was $250M more than expected. Additionally, adjusted EBIT increased to $583M, lifting adjusted EBIT expanded 20 basis points to 5.5%.

On the balance sheet, Magna International (NYSE:MGA) generated cash from operations before changes in operating assets and liabilities of $762M and used $135M in operating assets and liabilities. Investment activities in Q2 included $246M in fixed asset additions, $94M in investments, other assets and intangible assets, and $3M in private equity investments.

The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.485 per share, payable on August 29 to shareholders of record as of August 15.

For FY25, Magna (NYSE:MGA) modified its light vehicle production assumptions slightly, lowering the outlook in North America, unchanged in Europe, and slightly higher in China.

Accordingly, the company now expects total sales to be between $40.4B and $42B from a prior estimate of $40B to $41.6B, the midpoint of which is below the consensus estimate of $40.4B.

Adjusted EBIT margin was raised to 5.2%-5.6% from 5.1%-5.6% previously, while capital spending assumptions were lowered to $1.6B to $1.7B from earlier guidance of $1.7B to $1.8B.
